artifact.text:
## Task Breakdown — Login Story

### Backend Tasks
1. **FastAPI auth endpoint** (`POST /auth/login`)
   - Validate email+password against DB (bcrypt)
   - Issue JWT access token (1h) + refresh token (7d) in HttpOnly cookie
   - Rate-limit: 5 attempts / 15 min per IP; lock after 5 failures

2. **PostgreSQL user model + migration**
   - Table: `auth.users(id, email, password_hash, locked_until)`
   - Alembic migration, bcrypt on write path

### Frontend Tasks
3. **Angular login form component**
   - Reactive form: email + password + "Remember me" checkbox
   - Inline validation, clear error messages
   - Calls auth service on submit

4. **Auth service (Angular)**
   - POST /auth/login, store session token, handle 401/429

### Rationale
Decomposed the login story into two backend tasks (endpoint + DB) and two frontend tasks (form + service). The split keeps backend auth logic isolated from UI concerns, enabling parallel development and targeted evaluation at Level A.

tasks.title: FastAPI auth endpoint POST /auth/login
tasks.rationale: Core authentication logic; issues JWT tokens and enforces rate limiting

tasks.title: PostgreSQL user model and Alembic migration
tasks.rationale: Persistent storage for user credentials with bcrypt hashing

tasks.title: Angular login form component
tasks.rationale: User-facing reactive form with inline validation

tasks.title: Angular auth service
tasks.rationale: Client-side service to call the auth endpoint and manage session

structured_json.rationale: Split into backend (endpoint + DB) and frontend (form + service) to allow parallel development and isolated evaluation.
